steps.title: Target the claim to weaken
steps.content: The traditional view says voting does not change voters' attitudes toward candidates. A weakening finding must show that people who voted later differed in attitude from otherwise similar people who could not vote.

steps.title: Show voting changed attitudes
steps.content: Choice A makes attitudes depend on political orientation regardless of voting. Choice B shows change among nonvoters, not an effect of voting. Choice D reports a general negative tendency without isolating voting. A voter-versus-too-young comparison with more polarized voter attitudes weakens the claim. The answer is C.
